rulings.Case Title: MICKEY Y WONG VS. DORIS WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion/ Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 26, 2015, Line 1: Plaintiff Mickey Wong's Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint. GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Request is GRANTED to: (1) identification of new properties in relation to already alleged causes of action; (2) to add proposed new causes of action in regards to the new properties only. However, the inclusion of additional causes of action is DENIED as applied to previously known and ruled on properties. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BAY RIDGE PROPERTIES LLC VS. AMERICAN BROKERS CONDUIT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer And Notice Of Demurrer To Amended Cross Complaint In Intervention Pursuant To Cal. Code 1714.10
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 26, 2015, Line 2: CROSS DEFENDANT NIKHIL BHATNAGAR'S Demurrer To Amended Cross Complaint In Intervention Pursuant To Cal. Code 1714.10. Continued to July 24, 2015. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C and provide courtesy copy of the operative cross-complaint to Department 501 no later than June 30, 2015 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE JACKSON et al VS. KISHORE KAGOLANU et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 26, 2015, Line 3: CROSS DEFENDANT KATHRYN SHAFFER'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for cross-complainant to allege compliance with CCP 411.35 (Certificate of Merit). Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Cross-Defendant's reliance on Weseloh Family Partnership v. K.L.Wesel Construction (2004) 125 Cal. App. 4th 152 is premature. Weleoh family Partnership is a summary judgment case, where the Court after lengthy discussion of Biakanja factors found no duty of care existed, and based on that finding determined that indemnity claim necessarily failed. Here, Plaintiff alleges a cause of action for negligence. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY HANSON VS. JEFFREY APPENRODT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Of Moving Party'S Intention To Move Post Hearing On This Motion For An Award Of Contractual Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 26, 2015, Line 4: DEFENDANT JEFFREY APPENRODT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION and of Moving Party's Intention To Move Post Hearing On This Motion For An Award Of Contractual Attorneys Fees. DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). "If summary adjudication is sought, whether separately or as an alternative to the motion for summary judgment, the specific cause of action, affirmative defense, claims for damages, or issues of duty must be stated specifically in the notice of motion and be repeated, verbatim, in the separate statement of undisputed material facts." =(501/REQ)
